title: The Langs' troubling friendship with Jeffrey Epstein
subhed: Former French culture minister Jack Lang and his daughter Caroline Lang appear thousands of times in the most recently released documents. All evidence suggests that she had a much closer relationship with the sex offender than she now admits.

'Women are much more frequently victims of physical violence at work than men'
subhed: Violence in the workplace has serious consequences for both the victims and the perpetrators. However, within companies, there is a greater degree of impunity for male perpetrators of violence against women than in society at large, writes columnist Pauline Grosjean.

title: French minister Rachida Dati resigns to launch her Paris mayoral bid
subhed: The outspoken minister, who said she handed her resignation to French President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday morning, had survived a series of cabinet reshuffles since January 2024 despite facing corruption charges for which she will appear in court in the autumn.

title: Five Paris restaurants serving mouthwatering ramen
subhed: A staple of Japanese cuisine, this noodle soup takes on subtle flavors depending on whether it is made with pork, fish or vegetables. Here are our picks for the best places to enjoy excellent ramen in the French capital.

title: After four years of war in Ukraine, Russia's economy could fall into recession
subhed: The Russian economy, fueled by the country's military-industrial complex, had long posted growth rates above the European average. Today, however, the country's economic indicators are all in the red.

title: Donald Trump's support with 'bro culture' is waning

title: We fact-checked 'Marty Supreme,' the biopic of a not-so-visionary table tennis player
subhed: The biopic produced by Timothée Chalamet draws inspiration from the American champion Marty Reisman. But it was his Japanese rival, Hiroji Satoh, who brought the sport into the modern era.

title: 'The unnatural alliance between Trump and White evangelicals has cost them dearly, even if it allowed them to gain positions of power'
subhed: Historian Sébastien Fath discusses the history and dynamics of evangelical Protestant churches in an interview with Le Monde. Despite their current vibrancy, their growth is not inevitable, the researcher argues.
